data.title: Venture Global Stock Tumbles Almost 20%. It Just Lost This Bust Up With BP. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By George Glover

Venture Global stock was plummeting on Friday after the natural-gas exporter said it had lost an arbitration case against one of its own customers, BP.

Shares, which trade under the ticker VG, dropped 19% to $10.25 ahead of the opening bell. BP's American depositary receipts were 0.2% lower. Futures tracking the S&P 500 were flat.

The selloff came after Venture Global said in a regulatory filing that on Oct. 8, the International Court of Arbitration issued a partial final award to BP. The British oil major and several other customers have accused Venture Global of withholding contracted liquefied natural gas cargoes, and instead selling them on the spot market to benefit from a surge in prices that's been driven by the war in Ukraine.

BP is seeking damages of more than $1 billion, as well as interest, costs and attorneys' fees. Remedies will be determined in a separate damages hearing, which Venture Global said it expects will take place in 2026.

Venture Global has previously guided that it will report earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization of between $6.4 billion and $6.8 billion for the current year, so the ruling could end up being a significant blow to its profit.

The company went public via an initial public offering in January. Shares have tumbled 58% since then.

Write to George Glover at george.glover@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Delta Air Is Maintained at Buy by Deutsche Bank
India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at close
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex and the broader NSE Nifty 50 index both settled 0.4% higher at 82,500.82 points and 25,285.35 points, respectively, driven by heavyweight financials as prospects of foreign investors turning buyers and optimism ahead of quarterly earnings boosted sentiment.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose 0.1% versus the U.S. dollar at 88.6850, moving away from its all-time low after spending much of the week pinned near it, as persistent central bank defence of the currency spurred interbank dollar sales.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 98.54 rupees, with the yield up over 1 bps at 6.5370% with sentiment little changed after the auction and participants looking for strong triggers to revive appetite.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was up 2 basis points at 5.44%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) rose 3 bps to 5.6575%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 4.95% and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.27%.

data.title: Primary placement auction of additional offers of Lithuanian Government Eurobonds
data.description:
Nasdaq VilniusAnnouncement from the exchange

Primary placement auction of additional offers of Lithuanian GovernmentEurobonds

The Lithuanian Government Eurobonds auction of additional offers will be heldon 2025-10-20. Auction participants can submit competitive and non-competitivebids. The competitive bids shall be submitted in the order books of the VSE NewIssue Yield market, whereas the non-competitive bids shall be placed in theorder books of the VSE New Issue Yield Non market. The parameters of theemission to be issued shall be the following:

Type of security Lithuanian Government Eurobond --------------------------------------------------------------------------------ISIN code XS2979761769 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------Eurobond issue date 2025-01-28 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------Volume of issue before auction of additional offers, EUR 1515 m --------------------------------------------------------------------------------Volume of issue Is set during the auction --------------------------------------------------------------------------------Currency of issue EUR --------------------------------------------------------------------------------Nominal value, EUR 1000 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------Maturity, in days 1554 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------Payment date 2025-10-27 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------Redemption date 2030-01-28 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------The maximum volume of non-competitive bids, which may be 500 000 submitted by one auction participant, EUR* --------------------------------------------------------------------------------The cut-off yield, % Is not announced --------------------------------------------------------------------------------Coupon rate (annual interest rate) 2,875% --------------------------------------------------------------------------------Number of coupons per year 1 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------Coupon payment dates 2026-01-28; 2027-01-28; 2028-01-28; 2029-01-28; 2030-01-28. --------------------------------------------------------------------------------A competitive orderbook LTGCA287530A --------------------------------------------------------------------------------A non-competitive orderbook LTGNA287530A --------------------------------------------------------------------------------

* - With the written consent of the Ministry of Finance, this amount may beincreased by one additional amount, up to a maximum of EUR 5,000,000 for one ofthe auction participants.

The securities shall be redeemed by paying to their holders the total amount ofpar value. The paid up securities shall be admitted to the regulated market -to the Debt securities list of the Nasdaq Vilnius AB.

Nasdaq BalticTransaction Services+370 5 253 14 54www.nasdaqbaltic.com

Nasdaq Baltic is a common name for exchanges, regulated markets, alternativeFirst North markets operated by Nasdaq companies in the Baltic States, i.e.Nasdaq Tallinn AS, Nasdaq Riga AS and AB Nasdaq Vilnius.https://view.news.eu.nasdaq.com/view?id=b8bff0d4db274031ca3c32bffa67fc608&lang=en&src=omxlink

data.title: Serve Robotics retreats on $100 mln direct stock deal
data.description:
** Serve Robotics' shares down 7.6% at $16.33 premarket after delivery robot maker announces $100 mln equity raise

** Redwood City, California-headquartered SERV says it agreed to sell 6.25 mln shares to certain institutional investors in registered direct offering

** Purchase price equates to around $16, a roughly 9.5% discount to stock's last close

** SERV shares on Thurs soared ~29% to finish at $17.68 after co inked partnership with DoorDash to deploy its sidewalk robots to fulfill orders on the food delivery platform across the U.S., seeking to expand its reach beyond Uber Eats

** SERV plans to use net proceeds from the direct offering for general purposes, including working capital

** With ~59.88 mln shares outstanding, SERV has about $1.1 bln market cap

** Northland Capital Markets sole placement agent for the offering; Oppenheimer and Wedbush acting as capital markets advisors to SERV

** Through Thurs, SERV shares up ~31% YTD and have more than tripled over the past six months

** 6 of 7 analysts rate the stock "strong buy" or "buy", 1 rates "hold"; median PT is $16.50, per LSEG

About Haleon

Haleon (LSE/NYSE: HLN) is a global leader in consumer health, with a purpose to deliver better everyday health with humanity. Haleon's product portfolio spans six major categories - Oral Health, Vitamins, Minerals and Supplements (VMS), Pain Relief, Respiratory Health, Digestive Health and Therapeutic Skin Health and Other. Its long-standing brands - such as Advil, Centrum, Otrivin, Panadol, parodontax, Polident, Sensodyne, Theraflu and Voltaren - are built on trusted science, innovation and deep human understanding.

For more information, please visit www.haleon.com.

Endomines Finland Plc  www.endomines.com.  is a Finnish forerunner in sustainable mining. Our operations are focused on gold production and exploration in the Karelian Gold Line in Eastern Finland. We also own the rights to seven gold deposits in the US. We produce gold for the jewellery and electronics industries and create value by turning natural resources into wealth, and we also create value by transforming natural resources into wealth, an investment that can withstand the volatility of global politics. Our vision is to develop the Karelian gold line into one of the most important and sustainable gold-producing regions in the world. Endomines is listed on the OMX Helsinki main list (PAMPALO).

data.title: Mosaic says Q3 phosphate output below expectations, shares drop
data.description:
Fertilizer producer Mosaic said on Friday its third-quarter phosphate production was about 1.7 million tonnes, below its forecast following maintenance and asset restoration work earlier in the quarter.

Shares of the company fell 8.8% to $30.50 in premarket trading.

The U.S. fertilizer producer said it achieved its targeted run rate for several weeks in early August after major turnaround and asset restoration work.

However, mechanical issues at its Riverview sulfuric acid plant and utility interruptions at the Bartow facility in mid-September cut output for the rest of the month.

For the third quarter, Mosaic expected phosphate sales volumes of 1.8 to 2.0 million tonnes.

In the Mosaic Fertilizantes segment, third-quarter sales rose 25% from the prior quarter to 2.8 million tonnes, while potash production and sales were both about 2.3 million tonnes.

The Florida-based company is scheduled to report its third-quarter results on November 5.

data.title: Mankind Pharma signs ₹797-crore deal to acquire women's health portfolio from subsidiary BSV
data.description:
Drug maker Mankind Pharma Ltd on Friday (October 10) said it has executed a Business Transfer Agreement (BTA) with its wholly owned subsidiary, Bharat Serums and Vaccines Limited (BSV), for the acquisition of BSV’s Branded Generic Business relating to the Women Health Rx Portfolio on a going concern basis through a slump sale.

The transaction is valued at ₹797 crore, subject to closing adjustments. As per the terms of the agreement, 50% of the sale consideration will be paid on the Closing Date, as defined in the BTA, while the remaining amount will be payable within 150 days from the Closing Date.

The completion of the acquisition is contingent upon preconditions, closing actions, and other terms and conditions outlined in the agreement. Mankind Pharma noted that detailed disclosures were already provided in its earlier filing dated July 31, 2025.

First Quarter Results

Mankind Pharma reported an 18.1% year-on-year (YoY) decline in net profit at ₹444.6 crore for the first quarter, down from ₹543 crore in Q1FY25. However, revenue from operations surged 24.5% to ₹3,570 crore against ₹2,868 crore year-on-year.

At the operating level, EBITDA increased 26% to ₹847.6 crore in the first quarter of this fiscal year over ₹672.6 crore in the prior-year period. EBITDA margin improved slightly to 23.7% from 23.4%.

The company reported a 20.1% YoY fall in diluted earnings per share (EPS) to ₹10.6 for Q1FY26. Domestic revenue rose 18.9% to ₹3,101 crore, while export revenue surged 81.1% to ₹469 crore, aided by the consolidation of BSV and growth in the base business.

Mankind’s market share increased from 4.8% in March 2025 to 4.9% in June 2025, reflecting its continued outperformance relative to the Indian pharmaceutical market (IPM). For Q1FY26, secondary sales grew by 9.2% compared to IPM’s 8.6%.

Shares of Mankind Pharma Ltd ended at ₹2,464.00, down by ₹15.40, or 0.62%, on the BSE.

data.title: Gold Rises 1% After Slight Pullback — Market Talk
data.description: Gold prices regain ground in afternoon trade after pulling back slightly earlier in the session, with futures in New York rising 1% to $4,012.30 a troy ounce. "Dip-buyers re-emerged around $3,945, keeping the $4,000 area well defended for now," says Daniela Sabin Hathorn, senior analyst at Capital.com. "One near-term headwind to watch is dollar firmness; a stronger USD can cap the upside in gold even if the macro backdrop remains supportive." On Friday, the U.S. dollar index--which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies--is 0.2% lower at 99.36. Meanwhile, silver futures climb 2.8% to $48.49 an ounce, while platinum is up 0.3% at $1,661.90 an ounce.(giulia.petroni@wsj.com)
